discovery: Custom build with Next.js (web) + NestJS API + Postgres + Prisma + managed auth (Clerk/Auth0) + Stripe Billing
Fallback option includes WordPress + LearnDash/LifterLMS + Stripe add-ons with a custom service for payouts/reporting
User approved the primary custom stack choice.
Roles for initial launch: Admin, Student, Instructor.
Individual course purchases will be considered a later/optional phase.
Instructor payouts based on enrollments, either as a fixed amount per enrollment or a revenue-share per enrollment.
Payouts calculated monthly with an admin-approved payout run.
ui_ux: Subscription plans designed as a hybrid model: Monthly and Annual subscriptions for platform-level access, users can also buy individual courses as one-time purchases.
UI phase includes designing purchase flows and teacher earnings dashboards.
Question: should subscriptions unlock all courses on the platform, or only a subscription catalog?
features_journeys: Custom route supports subscription lifecycle + RBAC + instructor earnings/payout logic
Primary features for MVP: subscriptions, role-based access control (RBAC), course authoring, student progress tracking, certificate awarding, enrollment-based instructor payouts.
Month 1 - Foundation: RBAC for roles (Admin, Student, Instructor), course model development, publishing workflow, subscription plans (monthly/annual), payments and webhooks, core admin management.
Month 2 - Learning experience: catalog and search/filter, checkout, student dashboard, course player, progress tracking, email notifications.
Month 3 - Monetization and payouts: subscription lifecycle management, enrollment events, instructor earnings calculations, payout cycles and history, basic reporting.
Month 4 - Hardening and launch: QA, security measures, analytics, audit logs, support flows such as refunds and access overrides, performance optimization, legal compliance, and production readiness.
Admin Journey Details: User management, course oversight, monetization controls, enrollment and access management, payout operations, platform monitoring and analytics.
Student Journey Details: Discover courses, manage subscriptions, access course player, complete assessments, receive certificates, manage account.
Instructor Journey Details: Course creation and publishing, view course performance, earnings tracking, manage profile and payout methods.
Subscription lifecycle includes trial, upgrade, cancel, renew.

Core features for MVP: Role-based access, Course creation and publishing workflow, Enrollment and progress tracking, Certificates, Payout calculations, Notifications for emails regarding receipts, renewals, cancellations, and payouts.
Users should be able to pay for courses individually, in addition to subscriptions.
Teachers will be paid according to the number of people enrolled in their courses.
Platform to support course creation, subscription-based access, learning progress tracking, certifications, and payouts.
Centralized admin system to manage the platform.
tech_stack: Primary stack confirmed: Next.js + NestJS + Postgres + Prisma + Clerk/Auth0 + Stripe Billing + S3 + Mux/Cloudflare Stream + Postmark
Fallback stack capabilities: WordPress + LearnDash/LifterLMS + Stripe add-ons with a custom payout/reporting service
Confirmed tech stack: Frontend with Next.js (React) + Tailwind, Backend with NestJS (Node/TypeScript) REST API, Database with Postgres (managed) + Prisma ORM.
Authentication via Auth0 or Clerk (managed) with server-side RBAC.
Payments and subscriptions managed via Stripe Billing with an option for Stripe Connect for instructor payouts.
Storage with S3-compatible object storage (AWS S3) and video streaming via Mux or Cloudflare Stream.
Email and notifications via Postmark or SendGrid.
Infrastructure: Vercel for frontend, Render/Fly.io for API, managed Postgres.
budget_timeline: The project is planned for a 4-month timeline.
Delivery approach with specific monthly goals for the 4-month timeline.
open_questions: Should subscriptions unlock all courses on the platform, or only a subscription catalog?
